title: Bot Reply Keyboard (Persistent Custom Keyboards)
description: Bots can send a persistent reply keyboard that replaces the default text input panel for the duration of a flow. Unlike inline keyboards (attached to a specific message), reply keyboards are session-scoped and appear as a row of tappable buttons above the composer. Supports text buttons, request_contact, request_location, request_poll types.

ReplyKeyboardMarkup.keyboard: Button[][] — 2D array defining rows and columns
ReplyKeyboardMarkup.resize_keyboard: boolean — fit height to button count or fixed full height
ReplyKeyboardMarkup.input_field_placeholder: string (max 64 chars) — placeholder text in composer
ReplyKeyboardMarkup.selective: boolean — show keyboard only to mentioned users (groups)

KeyboardButton.text: string — displayed label AND value sent as message text
KeyboardButton.request_contact: boolean — sends phone number upon tap (requires user consent)
KeyboardButton.request_location: boolean — sends current location upon tap (requires permission)

web_app.url: string — opens Mini App in bounded iframe

schema.message_id: UUID FK → messages.id (message that injected this keyboard)

storage.rls: SELECT/UPDATE allowed only for bot_role owning bot_id AND user owning user_id. No cross-user visibility.
indexes: UNIQUE idx_brkb_active ON bot_reply_keyboards(user_id, conversation_id) WHERE dismissed_at IS NULL

transitions: INACTIVE → ACTIVE: bot sends message with ReplyKeyboardMarkup
ACTIVE → ONE_TIME_USED: user taps button AND one_time=true
ACTIVE → REMOVED: bot sends ReplyKeyboardRemove OR user dismisses manually
ONE_TIME_USED → INACTIVE: keyboard hidden from composer

server_enforced: Bot cannot set reply keyboard for a conversation where it has no active session (user has not started /start or bot is not member).
request_contact and request_location buttons: server must emit explicit user-consent challenge before transmitting sensitive data — client UX not sufficient.
Keyboard JSON validated server-side: max 100 buttons total, max 10 rows, max 10 buttons per row. Exceeding limits → 400 error.
XSS prevention: button text HTML-escaped at storage time; client renders as plain text only.
Rate limit: bot can update/set keyboard max 20 times/minute per user conversation.
attack_vectors: Malicious bot floods keyboard updates to drain client render budget → mitigated by rate limit + client debounce.
Homoglyph attack in button text to impersonate system actions → mitigated by Unicode normalization (NFC) on write.
request_contact bypass: bot tries to extract contact without user tap → server only emits contact on explicit user action tracked by server-side event.

api.send_message_with_keyboard: POST /v1/bots/{bot_id}/messages — reply_markup field accepts ReplyKeyboardMarkup JSON
api.keyboard_state_query: GET /v1/conversations/{conv_id}/keyboard?user_id={uid} — returns active keyboard for composer render (JWT scoped to user)

ui.composer_integration: When active keyboard exists: hide default keyboard, render button grid above safe area. 'Dismiss' chevron → sends ReplyKeyboardRemove equivalent.
ui.button_tap_flow: Tap → text value injected into message, sent immediately (no compose step for simple text buttons). request_contact/location → permission dialog first.
